acceptance_targets: [ch09_prose_001] PASS if at least two of the four cited lines no longer use voice-shake/crack language and still communicate escalation clearly; FAIL if all four retain the same vocal-distress cue family.
[ch09_prose_002] PASS if no more than one of the cited lines keeps the current abstract-noun + 'like a/an' impact metaphor pattern; FAIL if three or more of those lines still use the same template.

[ch09_prose_003] PASS if this revised corridor sequence contains at least three explicit interior markers from Ina and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interior experience; FAIL if the block still reads as external-only dialogue/action for 10+ paragraphs.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if at least two of the four cited lines no longer use voice-shake/crack language and still communicate escalation clearly; FAIL if all four retain the same vocal-distress cue family.
findings.problem: Stress is repeatedly signaled with the same vocal-collapse construction (voice shook/cracking/cracked/shaking). The recurrence becomes a mechanical default and flattens emotional texture.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:39, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:291, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:449, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:599 by replacing at least two voice-failure tags with non-redundant stress markers (breath pattern, gesture failure, lexical fracture, or sensory spill) specific to each speaker.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if no more than one of the cited lines keeps the current abstract-noun + 'like a/an' impact metaphor pattern; FAIL if three or more of those lines still use the same template.
findings.problem: A recurring metaphor template (abstract or procedural noun rendered as a blunt physical object via 'like a/an ...') appears repeatedly and starts to read as formula rather than choice.
findings.rewrite_direction: In snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:73, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:155, snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:401, and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:651, keep only the strongest metaphor instance and rewrite the others with scene-specific concrete action or sensory detail that does not rely on the same simile chassis.

findings.acceptance_test: PASS if this revised corridor sequence contains at least three explicit interior markers from Ina and no run longer than six consecutive paragraphs without interior experience; FAIL if the block still reads as external-only dialogue/action for 10+ paragraphs.
findings.problem: There is a 10+ paragraph screenplay-like stretch with no focalizer interiority, violating close-third pressure. The sedation corridor exchange runs on external dialogue/action only until later, weakening Ina-centered narrative distance.
findings.rewrite_direction: Within the corridor block anchored at snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:379 and snapshots/cycle_02/chapters/chapter_09.md:401, insert concise interior beats (thought/sensation/self-assessment) at entry, midpoint, and pre-breakpoint to keep close-third continuity without slowing pace.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Focus on prose and copyedit finish: diction precision, rhythm control, sentence-level clarity, grammar, line-level polish without flattening style, and explicit aesthetic-risk audit (sanitization is the primary risk — do not soften or euphemize; push toward creative risk and specificity).
revision_pass.label: prose/copyedit fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: Lean into uncomfortable specificity when stakes demand it: show coercion mechanics, sexual negotiation, grief rituals, and financial desperation at scene level. In edge cases, prefer precision over decorum; protect character dignity through perspective accuracy, not by omitting the hard material.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: If a scene requires bodily, economic, or moral consequence and the draft retreats into abstract phrasing, rewrite with concrete action, sensation, and decision impact before proceeding.
aesthetic_risk_policy.operational_resolution_rule: When uncertain between tasteful distance and narrative truth, choose the version that preserves causal accountability and embodied consequence, then tune language for clarity rather than softness.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: high - contractions are the baseline in spoken dialogue; uncontracted forms are reserved for deliberate emphasis, legal language, or character-specific formality.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate chapter structures deliberately: high-density dialogue vs interior reflection, multi-scene public collision vs two-scene intimate pressure, aftermath inventory vs anticipatory dread. Never let two consecutive chapters share the same pacing architecture or turn mechanism.
prose_style_profile.diction: Contemporary, unsentimental literary diction that permits technical vocabulary, profanity, and class-marked speech without flattening idiolects.
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: close third person centered on Seo Ina, with brief controlled widenings in public-event chapters.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Alternating pressure waves: compressed clauses in operational stress, longer breath-based lines in grief and reflection.
